page: OVHcloud d2-8 is a virtual server belonging to the Discovery family, built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Haswell processor running at 2.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 shared vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes a baseline bandwidth of 0 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate that the shared CPU allocation and older Haswell architecture limit performance. The single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the bottom 10% of tested servers, while multi-core performance sits in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds of 35.67 GB/sec and 23.03 GB/sec respectively, placing it in the bottom 25%. These metrics highlight a clear tradeoff: while the server provides local NVMe storage, its processing power and memory throughput are restricted.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d2-8 is positioned as an entry-level, budget-friendly resource. The use of shared vCPUs and older hardware components reduces resource density costs, making it suitable for non-production environments. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, basic software compilation, development environments, and low-throughput database testing where high-performance CPU and memory bandwidth are not critical.
description: The OVHcloud d2-8 is a KVM-based virtual server from the Discovery family, designed for entry-level workloads. It features 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel Haswell x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks indicate that the server operates in the lower tiers, with single-core CPU and memory scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% of tested instances. This performance profile reflects its shared CPU allocation and older hardware generation. Consequently, the d2-8 offers a budget-friendly option for non-critical tasks. It is best suited for lightweight applications, software development, testing environments, and basic static web serving.
og_description: OVHcloud d2-8 is a Discovery family virtual server featuring 4 shared Intel Haswell vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for budget-friendly development and testing workloads.

tagline: An entry-level virtual server with shared compute resources and local NVMe storage, optimized for budget-conscious development and testing.
bullet_points: Features 4 shared Intel Haswell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers entry-level performance suitable for non-production environments
Includes one complimentary public IPv4 address
Best suited for lightweight web serving, testing, and development tasks
